steps.title: Extract data from the table
steps.content: The baritone row shows 10 singers. The total number of singers is 4 + 6 + 10 + 5 = 25.

steps.title: Calculate the probability
steps.content:
The probability that a randomly selected singer is a baritone is:
$$P(\text{baritone}) = \frac{\text{number of baritones}}{\text{total singers}} = \frac{10}{25} = \frac{2}{5} = 0.4$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
